name: Alabama
funFact: Montgomery was the first capital of the Confederacy in 1861.

name: Alaska
funFact: Juneau is the only U.S. state capital with no road connection to the rest of the state.

name: Arizona
funFact: Phoenix is the most populous state capital in the United States.

name: Arkansas
funFact: Little Rock is named after a small rock formation on the Arkansas River.

name: California
funFact: Sacramento was a hub of the 1849 Gold Rush and California's first incorporated city.

name: Colorado
funFact: Denver is nicknamed the 'Mile-High City' because it sits exactly 5,280 feet above sea level.

name: Connecticut
funFact: Hartford is home to the oldest continuously published U.S. newspaper, the Hartford Courant.

name: Delaware
funFact: Delaware was the first state to ratify the U.S. Constitution, in 1787.

name: Florida
funFact: Tallahassee was chosen as capital because it sat halfway between St. Augustine and Pensacola.

name: Georgia
funFact: Atlanta hosted the 1996 Summer Olympics and is home to the world's busiest airport.

name: Hawaii
funFact: Honolulu is home to Iolani Palace, the only royal palace on U.S. soil.

name: Idaho
funFact: Boise's name likely comes from the French 'les bois', meaning 'the woods'.

name: Illinois
funFact: Springfield was home to Abraham Lincoln for nearly 25 years before his presidency.

name: Indiana
funFact: Indianapolis hosts the Indy 500, the largest single-day sporting event in the world.

name: Iowa
funFact: Des Moines holds the nation's first presidential caucuses every election cycle.

name: Kansas
funFact: Topeka was the setting of the landmark 1954 Brown v. Board of Education ruling.

name: Kentucky
funFact: Frankfort is one of the least populous state capitals in the U.S.

name: Louisiana
funFact: Baton Rouge means 'red stick' in French, named for a boundary marker on the river.

name: Maine
funFact: Maine is the only U.S. state whose name is just one syllable.

name: Maryland
funFact: Annapolis served briefly as the U.S. capital in 1783–84 and hosts the Naval Academy.

name: Massachusetts
funFact: Boston is home to the oldest public park in the U.S., Boston Common, founded in 1634.

name: Michigan
funFact: Lansing became the capital in 1847 to move government away from vulnerable Detroit.

name: Minnesota
funFact: Saint Paul and Minneapolis are known together as the Twin Cities.

name: Mississippi
funFact: Jackson is named after President Andrew Jackson.

name: Missouri
funFact: Jefferson City was purpose-built as a capital and named after Thomas Jefferson.

name: Montana
funFact: Helena was founded as a gold-mining camp called 'Last Chance Gulch'.

name: Nebraska
funFact: Lincoln's capitol is a 400-foot tower nicknamed 'The Tower on the Plains'.

name: Nevada
funFact: Carson City is named after frontiersman Kit Carson.

name: New Hampshire
funFact: Concord is a center of the granite industry, giving NH its 'Granite State' nickname.

name: New Jersey
funFact: Trenton briefly served as the capital of the United States in 1784.

name: New Mexico
funFact: Santa Fe, founded in 1610, is the oldest state capital in the United States.

name: New York
funFact: Albany is one of the oldest surviving European settlements from the original 13 colonies.

name: North Carolina
funFact: Raleigh is nicknamed the 'City of Oaks' for its many willow oak trees.

name: North Dakota
funFact: Bismarck was named to attract German railroad investment in the 1870s.

name: Ohio
funFact: Columbus is named after explorer Christopher Columbus.

name: Oklahoma
funFact: Oklahoma City grew to 10,000 people in a single day during the 1889 Land Run.

name: Oregon
funFact: Salem sits in the fertile Willamette Valley, a major wine and farming region.

name: Pennsylvania
funFact: Harrisburg was a key transportation hub on the Pennsylvania Canal and railroads.

name: Rhode Island
funFact: Providence was founded in 1636 by Roger Williams as a haven for religious freedom.

name: South Carolina
funFact: Columbia was one of the first planned cities in the United States, laid out in 1786.

name: South Dakota
funFact: Pierre (pronounced 'peer') is one of the smallest state capitals by population.

name: Tennessee
funFact: Nashville is known as 'Music City', home of country music and the Grand Ole Opry.

name: Texas
funFact: Austin's slogan is 'Keep Austin Weird' and it hosts the SXSW festival.

name: Utah
funFact: Salt Lake City was founded in 1847 by Mormon pioneers led by Brigham Young.

name: Vermont
funFact: Montpelier is the least populous state capital in the U.S. and has no McDonald's.

name: Virginia
funFact: Richmond was the capital of the Confederacy during most of the Civil War.

name: Washington
funFact: Olympia sits at the southern tip of Puget Sound, framed by the Olympic Mountains.

name: West Virginia
funFact: West Virginia split from Virginia in 1863 to remain in the Union during the Civil War.

name: Wisconsin
funFact: Madison is built on an isthmus between Lake Mendota and Lake Monona.

name: Wyoming
funFact: Wyoming was the first U.S. territory to grant women the right to vote, in 1869.
